source_jobs.title: Don't see the job you're looking for?
source_jobs.description:
Are you ready to build America’s energy future? Form Energy is an American manufacturing and energy technology company. We’re revolutionizing energy storage with cost-effective, multi-day technology designed to keep the electric grid secure and reliable, even during extended periods of stress. By strengthening the electric system and reimagining what’s possible, we’re giving clean energy a whole new form! 



In recent years, Form Energy has earned a number of accolades, including being named by TIME as a “Best Invention”, MIT Technology Review as a “Top Climate Tech Company To Watch”, and Fast Company as “One of the Next Big Things In Tech”. We are making rapid progress on our mission of delivering energy storage for a better world, and our team is growing just as rapidly to meet demand. We have signed contracts with leading electric utilities across the United States and production of our iron-air batteries is underway at our first high-volume manufacturing facility in West Virginia.



Working for Form Energy is more than just a job, it’s a chance to be part of something extraordinary. And now - right as we significantly scale up battery manufacturing -  might be the most exciting moment in the company’s history to join. We are assembling a team of highly talented and driven individuals across the country. Driven by our core values of humanity, excellence, and creativity, our team is determined to deliver on our mission and transform the energy landscape for the better.



Feeling energized to make a meaningful impact on the world? Then keep reading - you’ve come to the right place.





Interested in working at Form Energy but don't see an open role that matches your background? Please go ahead and submit your resume and when a role opens up that is a fit for you, we will be in touch!  



Curious to learn more about our first factory in Weirton, WV? Please visit our Form Factory webpage https://formenergy.com/form-factory-1/ for more information.



#LI-DNI



Humanity is a cornerstone of Form Energy’s culture, and we make sure our compensation and benefits reflect that. Form Energy offers competitive salaries, stock options, and a holistic benefits package to ensure all employees have what they need to thrive while working here. 



When it comes to you and your family’s health, we cover 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums for full-time employees - and 80% of healthcare premiums for dependents. This starts from day one. We also offer at least 12 weeks of paid leave for new parents (up to 20 weeks for birthing parents), and generous vacation policies to give employees time to recharge when needed. 



To build America’s energy future, we need everyone at the table. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply to our open jobs.




If you may require reasonable accommodations to participate in our interview process, please contact accommodations@formenergy.com. Requests for accommodations will be treated with discretion.

Form Energy is committed to maintaining the privacy of our applicants. Please be aware that we will never solicit sensitive personal information such as Social Security numbers or bank account details during the recruiting or hiring process.

ROLE DESCRIPTION

The Hardware and Systems Design Engineering team at Form is focused on designing the battery cell, module, and pack system that forms the foundation of our innovative utility-scale multi-day energy storage system. We strive to deliver designs that are optimized for performance, quality, reliability, and cost for high-volume manufacturing at Form Factory. Dimensional management is a key enabler to meeting these design objectives.



We're hiring an experienced Staff Mechanical Engineer reporting to the Lead Dimensional Engineer, you will serve as the primary technical partner and execution lead for dimensional engineering standards and interface controls across our hardware teams. This is a hands-on Staff-level role within the Dimensional Engineering team, designed for an expert who will own the tactical implementation of our dimensional strategy. You will act as the central support for standard operating procedures, driving the organizational adoption of ASME Y14.5-2018 and managing the standards process across all departments. If you are an experienced dimensional engineer who thrives on defining processes and ensuring high-quality design-to-manufacturing transitions, this is the role for you.



If this sounds like the right role for you, apply now! This is an exciting opportunity to be part of our multi-day energy storage system product launch at Form and contribute to a sustainable future.



Relocation assistance is available.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Process & Standards Ownership: Define and maintain the standard operating procedures for dimensional management, serving as the primary administrator of the engineering standards process

 - Standards Governance: Support the adoption and rigorous application of ASME Y14.5-2018 standards across Hardware, and Integration teams

 - Cross-Departmental Management: Own critical tolerance analysis workflows and drive the resolution of complex interface trade-offs to ensure manufacturability and serviceability

 - Mentorship: Act as an internal subject matter expert, coaching engineering teams on GD&T best practices




WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - Degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field; Bachelor's with 8+ years of industry experience in mechanical design, GD&T, interface control, and dimensional management

 - Proven ability to build, manage, and scale engineering standards

ROLE DESCRIPTION

The Production Controller is responsible for managing and optimizing production planning, scheduling, and reporting through Oracle Fusion Cloud. This role plays a key part in launching, maintaining, and enhancing Oracle Fusion modules that support manufacturing, inventory, and financial operations. The Production Controller ensures data integrity across departments and drives collaboration between Production, Supply Chain, Warehousing and Finance to achieve accurate costing, efficient resource planning, and strong operational performance.

This opportunity is based out of Form Factory 1 – Form Energy’s 550,000-square-foot, state-of-the-art manufacturing facility located in Weirton, WV, a commutable distance from Pittsburgh. Purpose-built for high-volume, advanced battery production, Form Factory 1 is a hub of fast-moving, collaborative work driving a new era of large-scale American energy manufacturing in the heart of the Rust Belt.







Relocation assistance is available.






WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Oracle Fusion ERP Implementation & Optimization
   
   - Lead and support the implementation and rollout of Oracle Fusion Cloud Manufacturing ensuring seamless integration with operational workflows.
   
   - Work with ERP project teams to define and configure production processes, routings, BOMs, and work centers in Oracle Fusion.
     Collaborate on data migration, validation, and testing to ensure accuracy of master data (items, cost structures, and production parameters).
   
   - Participate in User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and system validation for new modules, updates, and enhancements.
   
   - Continuously review system performance, identifying opportunities to streamline workflows and automate manual tasks using Oracle Fusion capabilities.
     

 - Production Control & Scheduling
   
   - Maintain accurate and up-to-date production data within Oracle Fusion, including work orders, materials, and resource utilization.
   
   - Monitor real-time production progress using Fusion dashboards and analytics tools.

 - Collaboration with Finance
   
   - Work closely with the Finance Department to ensure accurate cost tracking and reconciliation between production and financial modules in Oracle Fusion.
   
   - Support Finance in managing standard costing, work order variances, and cost roll-ups within the ERP system.

Form Energy is hiring a Staff Manufacturing Engineer to join our electrode processing team. You will play a vital role in translating novel research into high-quality manufacturing processes for our iron-air battery technology. Your work will focus on scaling and optimizing calendaring, drying, and slitting operations while collaborating closely with process engineers to ensure equipment designs meet technical requirements. This is an opportunity to directly impact the future of energy storage by owning the equipment lifecycle from initial selection through commissioning at our state-of-the-art Form Factory 1.



What you'll do:

 - Collaborate with the process engineering team to align equipment design with specific manufacturing needs.

 - Scale and optimize calendaring, drying, and slitting operations

 - Partner with procurement to identify and select high-performance equipment suppliers.

 - Lead the design, factory acceptance testing (FAT), and site acceptance testing (SAT) for calendaring and drying equipment.

 - Provide mentorship and practical guidance to technicians and junior engineers on equipment purchasing and commissioning cycles.

 - Prioritize safety and excellence while scaling initial process designs to full production volumes.



What you'll bring:

 - 7+ years of practical experience in process engineering within pilot lines or fast-paced manufacturing environments.

 - Proven track record of equipment purchasing, commissioning, and testing with a proactive approach to problem-solving.

 - Proficiency in statistical methods and tools such as JMP or Minitab for data-driven process development.

 - Deep expertise with industrial calendaring and drying processes.

 - Excellent communication skills and experience creating safety and quality documentation for best-in-class manufacturing practices.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Materials Science, or a related field.
   
   


WHERE YOU'LL WORK:

This role is based at Form Factory 1 in Weirton, WV—an easy commute from the city of Pittsburgh, PA. This 550,000-square-foot facility is our hub for high-volume battery production and a leader in the new era of American energy manufacturing. Relocation assistance is available.

ROLE DESCRIPTION

Form Energy is hiring a Senior Failure Analysis Technician on the Reliability team to conduct cell and module teardowns to facilitate failure analysis. As part of the Reliability team, you will work closely with engineers to complete detailed inspections of test articles that have undergone a variety of electrochemical, mechanical and environmental testing. This collaborative role combines hands-on work in the lab with data collection and documentation, providing key insights for product reliability.



This role provides an exciting opportunity to make a significant and daily impact on cutting-edge battery technology that will revolutionize the electric grid and bring major improvements to grid reliability and sustainability.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Work hands-on (manual drill, hand-held power tools, etc.) to conduct cell & module tear-downs critical to failure analysis.

 - Measure components' physical and electrical properties to determine if they’ve gone out of spec during operation.

 - Collect samples for submission to analytical testing.

 - Document inspection findings by summarizing the results in a database.

 - Maintain lab equipment, and be a champion of safety.

 - Coordinate with Engineers across a variety of disciplines, Facilities and Shipping/Receiving.

 - Perform device and component pre- and post-test analysis with high level of attention to detail, including visual inspections, leak tests, weld inspections, and electrical continuity checks.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - 7+ years experience as a technician in a technology field

 - Ability to lift up to 30 lbs

 - Experience using and maintaining tools and lab instruments

 - Comfortable working with chemicals and wearing PPE

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Experience with pre- and post-test analysis and inspections, and preparing reports of inspection results

 - Comfort using Gsuite tools (Gmail, Google Docs, etc.)
